title: Drive SwiftUI from a single source of truth
description: Store mutable UI state in @State/@StateObject and derive views from it, instead of duplicating or imperatively mutating view contents.
body: Use when building SwiftUI screens. Do not keep duplicate copies of the same state in multiple places, and do not try to mutate a view imperatively as in UIKit. Actions: own value state with @State and reference/model state with @StateObject (observed elsewhere via @ObservedObject/@EnvironmentObject), pass mutable access down as @Binding, and let the framework re-render when the source changes. Validation signals include the UI updating automatically on a state change with no manual refresh. Risk boundary: mutating a property that is not part of observable state means the view silently never updates (a stale-UI bug), while duplicating state risks divergence — keep one authoritative source per piece of state.
